title: Gooey Chilli Quedo Kenyan Stew
description: A sour and gooey Kenyan stew with a nostalgic twist, featuring tamarind and chilli powder. This recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner, serving 6 people in under 1 hour.
instructions: Heat the tadka oil in a cataplana over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chilli powder and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes, until they start to break down.
Add the tuna, coconut milk, fish sauce, salt, black pepper, and water. Stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Add the coriander leaves and scotch bonnet chilli. Stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for a further 5 minutes, until the stew is heated through.
Serve hot, garnished with additional coriander leaves if desired.
Garnish with scotch bonnet chilli if desired.
Serve with rice or roti, if desired.
